search_text: Using Deep Learning-based Features Extracted from CT scans to Predict Outcomes in COVID-19 Patients 使用从 CT 扫描中提取的基于深度学习的特征来预测 COVID-19 患者的结果 The COVID-19 pandemic has had a considerable impact on day-to-day life. Tackling the disease by providing the necessary resources to the affected is of paramount importance. However, estimation of the required resources is not a trivial task given the number of factors which determine the requirement. This issue can be addressed by predicting the probability that an infected patient requires Intensive Care Unit (ICU) support and the importance of each of the factors that influence it. Moreover, to assist the doctors in determining the patients at high risk of fatality, the probability of death is also calculated. For determining both the patient outcomes (ICU admission and death), a novel methodology is proposed by combining multi-modal features, extracted from Computed Tomography (CT) scans and Electronic Health Record (EHR) data. Deep learning models are leveraged to extract quantitative features from CT scans. These features combined with those directly read from the EHR database are fed into machine learning models to eventually output the probabilities of patient outcomes. This work demonstrates both the ability to apply a broad set of deep learning methods for general quantification of Chest CT scans and the ability to link these quantitative metrics to patient outcomes. The effectiveness of the proposed method is shown by testing it on an internally curated dataset, achieving a mean area under Receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.77 on ICU admission prediction and a mean AUC of 0.73 on death prediction using the best performing classifiers. COVID-19 大流行对日常生活产生了相当大的影响。通过向受影响者提供必要的资源来应对这种疾病至关重要。然而，考虑到决定需求的因素有很多，估计所需资源并不是一项简单的任务。这个问题可以通过预测受感染患者需要重症监护病房 (ICU) 支持的概率以及影响它的每个因素的重要性来解决。此外，为了协助医生确定死亡高危患者，还计算了死亡概率。为了确定患者的结果（ICU 入院和死亡），通过结合从计算机断层扫描 (CT) 扫描和电子健康记录 (EHR) 数据中提取的多模态特征，提出了一种新的方法。利用深度学习模型从 CT 扫描中提取定量特征。这些特征与直接从 EHR 数据库读取的特征相结合，被输入机器学习模型，最终输出患者结果的概率。这项工作展示了应用广泛的深度学习方法对胸部 CT 扫描进行一般量化的能力，以及将这些定量指标与患者结果联系起来的能力。通过在内部管理的数据集上进行测试，证明了所提出方法的有效性，使用性能最佳的分类器，ICU 入院预测的接收者操作特征曲线 (AUC) 下的平均面积 (AUC) 为 0.77，死亡预测的平均 AUC 为 0.73。 本文提出一种结合CT扫描深度学习特征与电子健康记录（EHR）数据的方法，用于预测COVID-19患者的ICU入院和死亡概率。 Sai Vidyaranya Nuthalapati,Marcela Vizcaychipi,Pallav Shah,Piotr Chudzik,Chee Hau Leow,Paria Yousefi,Ahmed Selim,Keiran Tait,Ben Irving eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Phuc Thai,My T. Thai,Tam Vu,Thang N. Dinh
title: FastHare: Fast Hamiltonian Reduction for Large-scale Quantum Annealing
title_zh: FastHare：大规模量子退火的快速哈密顿约简
abstract: Quantum annealing (QA) that encodes optimization problems into Hamiltonians remains the only near-term quantum computing paradigm that provides sufficient many qubits for real-world applications. To fit larger optimization instances on existing quantum annealers, reducing Hamiltonians into smaller equivalent Hamiltonians provides a promising approach. Unfortunately, existing reduction techniques are either computationally expensive or ineffective in practice. To this end, we introduce a novel notion of non-separable~group, defined as a subset of qubits in a Hamiltonian that obtains the same value in optimal solutions. We develop a theoretical framework on non-separability accordingly and propose FastHare, a highly efficient reduction method. FastHare, iteratively, detects and merges non-separable groups into single qubits. It does so within a provable worst-case time complexity of only $O(αn^2)$, for some user-defined parameter $α$. Our extensive benchmarks for the feasibility of the reduction are done on both synthetic Hamiltonians and 3000+ instances from the MQLIB library. The results show FastHare outperforms the roof duality, the implemented reduction method in D-Wave's SDK library, with 3.6x higher average reduction ratio. It demonstrates a high level of effectiveness with an average of 62% qubits saving and 0.3s processing time, advocating for Hamiltonian reduction as an inexpensive necessity for QA.
summary: Quantum annealing (QA) that encodes optimization problems into Hamiltonians remains the only near-term quantum computing paradigm that provides sufficient many qubits for real-world applications. To fit larger optimization instances on existing quantum annealers, reducing Hamiltonians into smaller equivalent Hamiltonians provides a promising approach. Unfortunately, existing reduction techniques are either computationally expensive or ineffective in practice. To this end, we introduce a novel notion of non-separable~group, defined as a subset of qubits in a Hamiltonian that obtains the same value in optimal solutions. We develop a theoretical framework on non-separability accordingly and propose FastHare, a highly efficient reduction method. FastHare, iteratively, detects and merges non-separable groups into single qubits. It does so within a provable worst-case time complexity of only $O(αn^2)$, for some user-defined parameter $α$. Our extensive benchmarks for the feasibility of the reduction are done on both synthetic Hamiltonians and 3000+ instances from the MQLIB library. The results show FastHare outperforms the roof duality, the implemented reduction method in D-Wave's SDK library, with 3.6x higher average reduction ratio. It demonstrates a high level of effectiveness with an average of 62% qubits saving and 0.3s processing time, advocating for Hamiltonian reduction as an inexpensive necessity for QA.
abstract_zh: 将优化问题编码为哈密顿量的量子退火 (QA) 仍然是近期唯一为现实应用提供足够多量子位的量子计算范例。为了在现有的量子退火器上适应更大的优化实例，将哈密顿量减少为更小的等效哈密顿量提供了一种有前途的方法。不幸的是，现有的缩减技术要么计算成本昂贵，要么在实践中无效。为此，我们引入了不可分离组的新概念，定义为哈密顿量中的量子位的子集，它在最优解中获得相同的值。我们相应地开发了不可分离性的理论框架，并提出了 FastHare，一种高效的约简方法。 FastHare 迭代地检测不可分离的组并将其合并为单个量子位。对于某些用户定义的参数 $α$，它在可证明的最坏情况时间复杂度仅为 $O(αn^2)$ 内完成此操作。我们对减少可行性的广泛基准是在合成哈密顿量和 MQLIB 库中的 3000 多个实例上完成的。结果表明，FastHare 的性能优于屋顶对偶性（D-Wave SDK 库中实现的缩减方法），平均缩减率高出 3.6 倍。它展示了高水平的有效性，平均节省了 62% 的量子比特和 0.3 秒的处理时间，提倡将哈密顿量约简作为 QA 的廉价必需品。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FastHare方法，通过高效约简哈密顿量规模以提升大规模量子退火的可行性。
AI.motivation: 现有哈密顿量约简技术存在计算成本高或效果差的问题，限制了量子退火在实际优化问题中的应用。
AI.method: 基于非分离组理论框架，提出FastHare算法迭代合并等效量子比特组，时间复杂度为O(αn²)，并验证其在合成与真实数据集上的有效性。
AI.result: FastHare在3000+测试实例中实现平均62%量子比特节省，处理时间仅0.3秒，较D-Wave方法提升3.6倍。
AI.conclusion: 哈密顿量约简应成为量子退火的必要预处理步骤，FastHare为大规模应用提供了高效解决方案。

title: Improving Emergency Training for Earthquakes Through Immersive Virtual Environments and Anxiety Tests: A Case Study
title_zh: 通过沉浸式虚拟环境和焦虑测试改进地震应急训练：案例研究
comment: ef:A Case Study. Buildings, 12(11), 1850 (2022)
comments: ef:A Case Study. Buildings, 12(11), 1850 (2022)
abstract: Because of the occurrence of severe and large magnitude earthquakes each year, earthquake-prone countries suffer considerable financial damage and loss of life. Teaching essential safety measures will lead to a generation that can perform basic procedures during an earthquake, which is an important and effective solution in preventing the loss of life in this natural disaster. In recent years, virtual reality technology is a tool that has been used to educate people on safety matters. This paper evaluates the effect of education and premonition on the incorrect decision-making of residents under the stressful conditions of an earthquake. For this purpose, a virtual model has been designed and built from a proposed classroom in a school of the city of Tehran. Accordingly, two educational scenarios, presented in reality and the virtual model respectively, were conducted on a statistical sample of 20 students within the range of 20 to 25 years of age. Within the mentioned sample, the first group of 10 students were taught safety measures in a traditional classroom. The second group of 10 students participated in a virtual classroom. Evaluation tests on safety measures against earthquakes were distributed after two weeks. Furthermore, two self-reporting tests of Depression, anxiety, stress test (DASS), and Beck Anxiety Inventory (BAI) were assigned to the second group to evaluate the effect of foresight under two different scenarios. The results show that educating through virtual reality technology yields a higher performance level relative to the traditional approach to education. Additionally, the ability to detect earthquakes ahead of time is an influential factor in controlling stress and determining the right decisions should the event occur.

abstract_zh: 由于每年都会发生强烈、特大地震，地震多发国家遭受了相当大的经济损失和人员伤亡。教授基本安全措施将使一代人能够在地震期间执行基本程序，这是防止这场自然灾害中人员伤亡的重要而有效的解决方案。近年来，虚拟现实技术已成为一种用于教育人们安全问题的工具。本文评估了地震应激条件下教育和预警对居民错误决策的影响。为此，我们在德黑兰市一所学校的拟议教室中设计并构建了一个虚拟模型。据此，对20名20岁至25岁的学生进行统计样本，分别呈现现实和虚拟模型的两种教育场景。在上述样本中，第一批 10 名学生在传统教室中接受了安全措施教育。第二组 10 名学生参加了虚拟课堂。两周后分发了抗震安全措施的评估测试。此外，第二组还被分配了抑郁、焦虑、压力测试（DASS）和贝克焦虑量表（BAI）两项自我报告测试，以评估两种不同情景下远见的效果。结果表明，相对于传统的教育方法，通过虚拟现实技术进行的教育可以产生更高的绩效水平。此外，提前探测地震的能力是控制压力和在事件发生时做出正确决策的影响因素。

AI.tldr: 本文通过虚拟现实技术和焦虑测试评估地震应急训练效果，对比传统教学与虚拟场景教学的差异。
AI.motivation: 地震频发导致重大人员伤亡和财产损失，传统安全教育效果有限，需探索更有效的应急训练方法。
AI.method: 构建德黑兰学校教室的虚拟模型，将20名学生分为两组（传统课堂与虚拟课堂）进行安全教育，两周后通过知识测试及焦虑量表（DASS、BAI）评估效果。
AI.result: 虚拟现实教学组在安全知识测试中表现优于传统组，且预知地震能力与压力控制及决策准确性呈正相关。
AI.conclusion: 虚拟现实技术能显著提升地震应急训练效果，预知能力的培养对降低焦虑和优化应急决策具有重要作用。

method: 构建德黑兰学校教室的虚拟模型，将20名学生分为两组（传统课堂与虚拟课堂）进行安全教育，两周后通过知识测试及焦虑量表（DASS、BAI）评估效果。
result: 虚拟现实教学组在安全知识测试中表现优于传统组，且预知地震能力与压力控制及决策准确性呈正相关。
conclusion: 虚拟现实技术能显著提升地震应急训练效果，预知能力的培养对降低焦虑和优化应急决策具有重要作用。
search_text: Improving Emergency Training for Earthquakes Through Immersive Virtual Environments and Anxiety Tests: A Case Study 通过沉浸式虚拟环境和焦虑测试改进地震应急训练：案例研究 Because of the occurrence of severe and large magnitude earthquakes each year, earthquake-prone countries suffer considerable financial damage and loss of life. Teaching essential safety measures will lead to a generation that can perform basic procedures during an earthquake, which is an important and effective solution in preventing the loss of life in this natural disaster. In recent years, virtual reality technology is a tool that has been used to educate people on safety matters. This paper evaluates the effect of education and premonition on the incorrect decision-making of residents under the stressful conditions of an earthquake. For this purpose, a virtual model has been designed and built from a proposed classroom in a school of the city of Tehran. Accordingly, two educational scenarios, presented in reality and the virtual model respectively, were conducted on a statistical sample of 20 students within the range of 20 to 25 years of age. Within the mentioned sample, the first group of 10 students were taught safety measures in a traditional classroom. The second group of 10 students participated in a virtual classroom. Evaluation tests on safety measures against earthquakes were distributed after two weeks. Furthermore, two self-reporting tests of Depression, anxiety, stress test (DASS), and Beck Anxiety Inventory (BAI) were assigned to the second group to evaluate the effect of foresight under two different scenarios. The results show that educating through virtual reality technology yields a higher performance level relative to the traditional approach to education. Additionally, the ability to detect earthquakes ahead of time is an influential factor in controlling stress and determining the right decisions should the event occur. 由于每年都会发生强烈、特大地震，地震多发国家遭受了相当大的经济损失和人员伤亡。教授基本安全措施将使一代人能够在地震期间执行基本程序，这是防止这场自然灾害中人员伤亡的重要而有效的解决方案。近年来，虚拟现实技术已成为一种用于教育人们安全问题的工具。本文评估了地震应激条件下教育和预警对居民错误决策的影响。为此，我们在德黑兰市一所学校的拟议教室中设计并构建了一个虚拟模型。据此，对20名20岁至25岁的学生进行统计样本，分别呈现现实和虚拟模型的两种教育场景。在上述样本中，第一批 10 名学生在传统教室中接受了安全措施教育。第二组 10 名学生参加了虚拟课堂。两周后分发了抗震安全措施的评估测试。此外，第二组还被分配了抑郁、焦虑、压力测试（DASS）和贝克焦虑量表（BAI）两项自我报告测试，以评估两种不同情景下远见的效果。结果表明，相对于传统的教育方法，通过虚拟现实技术进行的教育可以产生更高的绩效水平。此外，提前探测地震的能力是控制压力和在事件发生时做出正确决策的影响因素。 本文通过虚拟现实技术和焦虑测试评估地震应急训练效果，对比传统教学与虚拟场景教学的差异。 Mohammad Sadra Rajabi,Hosein Taghaddos,Mehdi Zahrai cs.HC,cs.CY

authors_text: Marko Mihajlovic,Aayush Bansal,Michael Zollhoefer,Siyu Tang,Shunsuke Saito
title: KeypointNeRF: Generalizing Image-based Volumetric Avatars using Relative Spatial Encoding of Keypoints
title_zh: KeypointNeRF：使用关键点的相对空间编码来概括基于图像的体积头像
comment: To appear at ECCV 2022. The project page is available at https://markomih.github.io/KeypointNeRF
comments: To appear at ECCV 2022. The project page is available at https://markomih.github.io/KeypointNeRF
abstract: Image-based volumetric humans using pixel-aligned features promise generalization to unseen poses and identities. Prior work leverages global spatial encodings and multi-view geometric consistency to reduce spatial ambiguity. However, global encodings often suffer from overfitting to the distribution of the training data, and it is difficult to learn multi-view consistent reconstruction from sparse views. In this work, we investigate common issues with existing spatial encodings and propose a simple yet highly effective approach to modeling high-fidelity volumetric humans from sparse views. One of the key ideas is to encode relative spatial 3D information via sparse 3D keypoints. This approach is robust to the sparsity of viewpoints and cross-dataset domain gap. Our approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods for head reconstruction. On human body reconstruction for unseen subjects, we also achieve performance comparable to prior work that uses a parametric human body model and temporal feature aggregation. Our experiments show that a majority of errors in prior work stem from an inappropriate choice of spatial encoding and thus we suggest a new direction for high-fidelity image-based human modeling. https://markomih.github.io/KeypointNeRF
summary: Image-based volumetric humans using pixel-aligned features promise generalization to unseen poses and identities. Prior work leverages global spatial encodings and multi-view geometric consistency to reduce spatial ambiguity. However, global encodings often suffer from overfitting to the distribution of the training data, and it is difficult to learn multi-view consistent reconstruction from sparse views. In this work, we investigate common issues with existing spatial encodings and propose a simple yet highly effective approach to modeling high-fidelity volumetric humans from sparse views. One of the key ideas is to encode relative spatial 3D information via sparse 3D keypoints. This approach is robust to the sparsity of viewpoints and cross-dataset domain gap. Our approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods for head reconstruction. On human body reconstruction for unseen subjects, we also achieve performance comparable to prior work that uses a parametric human body model and temporal feature aggregation. Our experiments show that a majority of errors in prior work stem from an inappropriate choice of spatial encoding and thus we suggest a new direction for high-fidelity image-based human modeling. https://markomih.github.io/KeypointNeRF
abstract_zh: 使用像素对齐特征的基于图像的体积人体有望泛化到看不见的姿势和身份。先前的工作利用全局空间编码和多视图几何一致性来减少空间模糊性。然而，全局编码经常遭受对训练数据分布的过度拟合，并且很难从稀疏视图中学习多视图一致重建。在这项工作中，我们研究了现有空间编码的常见问题，并提出了一种简单但高效的方法来从稀疏视图中建模高保真体积人体。关键思想之一是通过稀疏 3D 关键点对相对空间 3D 信息进行编码。这种方法对于观点的稀疏性和跨数据集域差距是鲁棒的。我们的方法优于最先进的头部重建方法。在对看不见的受试者进行人体重建方面，我们也取得了与使用参数化人体模型和时间特征聚合的先前工作相当的性能。我们的实验表明，先前工作中的大多数错误源于空间编码选择不当，因此我们提出了基于高保真图像的人体建模的新方向。 https://markomih.github.io/KeypointNeRF

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为KeypointNeRF的方法，用于从稀疏视角中生成高保真度的体素人体模型。
AI.motivation: 现有的基于图像的体素人体建模方法在稀疏视角下难以实现多视角一致的重建，并且全局空间编码容易过拟合训练数据。
AI.method: 该方法通过稀疏的3D关键点对相对空间信息进行编码，从而增强模型对视角稀疏性和跨数据集域差距的鲁棒性。
AI.result: 该方法在头部重建任务中优于现有最先进的方法，并在未见过的主体的人体重建任务中表现与使用参数化人体模型和时间特征聚合的方法相当。
AI.conclusion: 本文认为，现有方法的大部分误差源于空间编码的选择不当，并提出了一个新方向，以实现高保真度的基于图像的人体建模。

authors_text: Lianrui Zuo,Yihao Liu,Yuan Xue,Shuo Han,Murat Bilgel,Susan M. Resnick,Jerry L. Prince,Aaron Carass
title: Disentangling A Single MR Modality
title_zh: 理清单一 MR 模式
abstract: Disentangling anatomical and contrast information from medical images has gained attention recently, demonstrating benefits for various image analysis tasks. Current methods learn disentangled representations using either paired multi-modal images with the same underlying anatomy or auxiliary labels (e.g., manual delineations) to provide inductive bias for disentanglement. However, these requirements could significantly increase the time and cost in data collection and limit the applicability of these methods when such data are not available. Moreover, these methods generally do not guarantee disentanglement. In this paper, we present a novel framework that learns theoretically and practically superior disentanglement from single modality magnetic resonance images. Moreover, we propose a new information-based metric to quantitatively evaluate disentanglement. Comparisons over existing disentangling methods demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance in both disentanglement and cross-domain image-to-image translation tasks.
summary: Disentangling anatomical and contrast information from medical images has gained attention recently, demonstrating benefits for various image analysis tasks. Current methods learn disentangled representations using either paired multi-modal images with the same underlying anatomy or auxiliary labels (e.g., manual delineations) to provide inductive bias for disentanglement. However, these requirements could significantly increase the time and cost in data collection and limit the applicability of these methods when such data are not available. Moreover, these methods generally do not guarantee disentanglement. In this paper, we present a novel framework that learns theoretically and practically superior disentanglement from single modality magnetic resonance images. Moreover, we propose a new information-based metric to quantitatively evaluate disentanglement. Comparisons over existing disentangling methods demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance in both disentanglement and cross-domain image-to-image translation tasks.
abstract_zh: 从医学图像中解开解剖和对比信息最近引起了人们的关注，这证明了对各种图像分析任务的好处。当前的方法使用具有相同基础解剖结构的成对多模态图像或辅助标签（例如，手动描绘）来学习解缠结表示，以提供解缠结的归纳偏差。然而，这些要求可能会显着增加数据收集的时间和成本，并在无法获得此类数据时限制这些方法的适用性。此外，这些方法通常不能保证解开。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的框架，可以从理论上和实践上学习单模态磁共振图像的优越解缠结。此外，我们提出了一种新的基于信息的指标来定量评估解缠结。与现有解缠结方法的比较表明，所提出的方法在解缠结和跨域图像到图像转换任务中均取得了优异的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种从单一磁共振模态图像中学习解耦解剖与对比信息的新框架，并设计信息度量指标评估解耦效果。
AI.motivation: 现有解耦方法依赖多模态配对数据或辅助标签，导致数据获取成本高且适用性受限，且无法保证解耦质量。
AI.method: 构建无需多模态数据的单模态解耦框架，结合理论分析与信息熵指标量化评估解耦程度。
AI.result: 在解耦性能及跨域图像翻译任务中均优于现有方法，验证了框架有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法突破传统解耦对多模态数据的依赖，在医疗图像分析中具有更广适用性。

authors_text: Shujian Zhang,Chengyue Gong,Xingchao Liu,Pengcheng He,Weizhu Chen,Mingyuan Zhou
title: ALLSH: Active Learning Guided by Local Sensitivity and Hardness
title_zh: ALLSH：以局部敏感性和硬度为指导的主动学习
comment: NAACL 2022 (finding); Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/szhang42/allsh
comments: NAACL 2022 (finding); Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/szhang42/allsh
abstract: Active learning, which effectively collects informative unlabeled data for annotation, reduces the demand for labeled data. In this work, we propose to retrieve unlabeled samples with a local sensitivity and hardness-aware acquisition function. The proposed method generates data copies through local perturbations and selects data points whose predictive likelihoods diverge the most from their copies. We further empower our acquisition function by injecting the select-worst case perturbation. Our method achieves consistent gains over the commonly used active learning strategies in various classification tasks. Furthermore, we observe consistent improvements over the baselines on the study of prompt selection in prompt-based few-shot learning. These experiments demonstrate that our acquisition guided by local sensitivity and hardness can be effective and beneficial for many NLP tasks.
summary: Active learning, which effectively collects informative unlabeled data for annotation, reduces the demand for labeled data. In this work, we propose to retrieve unlabeled samples with a local sensitivity and hardness-aware acquisition function. The proposed method generates data copies through local perturbations and selects data points whose predictive likelihoods diverge the most from their copies. We further empower our acquisition function by injecting the select-worst case perturbation. Our method achieves consistent gains over the commonly used active learning strategies in various classification tasks. Furthermore, we observe consistent improvements over the baselines on the study of prompt selection in prompt-based few-shot learning. These experiments demonstrate that our acquisition guided by local sensitivity and hardness can be effective and beneficial for many NLP tasks.
abstract_zh: 主动学习可以有效地收集信息丰富的未标记数据进行注释，减少对标记数据的需求。在这项工作中，我们建议使用局部灵敏度和硬度感知采集功能来检​​索未标记的样本。所提出的方法通过局部扰动生成数据副本，并选择预测可能性与其副本差异最大的数据点。我们通过注入选择最坏情况的扰动来进一步增强我们的采集功能。我们的方法在各种分类任务中比常用的主动学习策略取得了一致的收益。此外，我们观察到基于提示的小样本学习中的提示选择研究相对于基线的持续改进。这些实验表明，我们以局部敏感性和硬度为指导的采集对于许多 NLP 任务来说是有效且有益的。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于局部敏感度和难度的主动学习方法，以高效选择未标记数据进行标注，减少对标注数据的需求。
AI.motivation: 传统主动学习方法在选择未标记数据时可能忽略数据的局部敏感性和难度，导致信息获取效率低下，因此需要一种更有效的方法来提升标注效率和模型性能。
AI.method: 通过局部扰动生成数据副本，计算数据点与副本的预测可能性差异，选择差异最大的样本；并引入最坏情况扰动以增强获取函数的鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在多种分类任务及基于提示的少样本学习中，该方法在提示选择任务上均优于现有基线方法，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 基于局部敏感度和难度的主动学习方法在多种NLP任务中表现优异，为减少标注需求提供了有效解决方案。

search_text: ALLSH: Active Learning Guided by Local Sensitivity and Hardness ALLSH：以局部敏感性和硬度为指导的主动学习 Active learning, which effectively collects informative unlabeled data for annotation, reduces the demand for labeled data. In this work, we propose to retrieve unlabeled samples with a local sensitivity and hardness-aware acquisition function. The proposed method generates data copies through local perturbations and selects data points whose predictive likelihoods diverge the most from their copies. We further empower our acquisition function by injecting the select-worst case perturbation. Our method achieves consistent gains over the commonly used active learning strategies in various classification tasks. Furthermore, we observe consistent improvements over the baselines on the study of prompt selection in prompt-based few-shot learning. These experiments demonstrate that our acquisition guided by local sensitivity and hardness can be effective and beneficial for many NLP tasks. 主动学习可以有效地收集信息丰富的未标记数据进行注释，减少对标记数据的需求。在这项工作中，我们建议使用局部灵敏度和硬度感知采集功能来检​​索未标记的样本。所提出的方法通过局部扰动生成数据副本，并选择预测可能性与其副本差异最大的数据点。我们通过注入选择最坏情况的扰动来进一步增强我们的采集功能。我们的方法在各种分类任务中比常用的主动学习策略取得了一致的收益。此外，我们观察到基于提示的小样本学习中的提示选择研究相对于基线的持续改进。这些实验表明，我们以局部敏感性和硬度为指导的采集对于许多 NLP 任务来说是有效且有益的。 提出一种基于局部敏感度和难度的主动学习方法，以高效选择未标记数据进行标注，减少对标注数据的需求。 Shujian Zhang,Chengyue Gong,Xingchao Liu,Pengcheng He,Weizhu Chen,Mingyuan Zhou cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Yu-Jie Yuan,Yang-Tian Sun,Yu-Kun Lai,Yuewen Ma,Rongfei Jia,Lin Gao
title: NeRF-Editing: Geometry Editing of Neural Radiance Fields
title_zh: NeRF-Editing：神经辐射场的几何编辑
comment: Accepted by CVPR 2022
comments: Accepted by CVPR 2022
abstract: Implicit neural rendering, especially Neural Radiance Field (NeRF), has shown great potential in novel view synthesis of a scene. However, current NeRF-based methods cannot enable users to perform user-controlled shape deformation in the scene. While existing works have proposed some approaches to modify the radiance field according to the user's constraints, the modification is limited to color editing or object translation and rotation. In this paper, we propose a method that allows users to perform controllable shape deformation on the implicit representation of the scene, and synthesizes the novel view images of the edited scene without re-training the network. Specifically, we establish a correspondence between the extracted explicit mesh representation and the implicit neural representation of the target scene. Users can first utilize well-developed mesh-based deformation methods to deform the mesh representation of the scene. Our method then utilizes user edits from the mesh representation to bend the camera rays by introducing a tetrahedra mesh as a proxy, obtaining the rendering results of the edited scene. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework can achieve ideal editing results not only on synthetic data, but also on real scenes captured by users.
summary: Implicit neural rendering, especially Neural Radiance Field (NeRF), has shown great potential in novel view synthesis of a scene. However, current NeRF-based methods cannot enable users to perform user-controlled shape deformation in the scene. While existing works have proposed some approaches to modify the radiance field according to the user's constraints, the modification is limited to color editing or object translation and rotation. In this paper, we propose a method that allows users to perform controllable shape deformation on the implicit representation of the scene, and synthesizes the novel view images of the edited scene without re-training the network. Specifically, we establish a correspondence between the extracted explicit mesh representation and the implicit neural representation of the target scene. Users can first utilize well-developed mesh-based deformation methods to deform the mesh representation of the scene. Our method then utilizes user edits from the mesh representation to bend the camera rays by introducing a tetrahedra mesh as a proxy, obtaining the rendering results of the edited scene. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework can achieve ideal editing results not only on synthetic data, but also on real scenes captured by users.
abstract_zh: 隐式神经渲染，尤其是神经辐射场（NeRF），在场景的新颖视图合成中显示出了巨大的潜力。然而，当前基于 NeRF 的方法无法使用户在场景中执行用户控制的形状变形。虽然现有的工作提出了一些根据用户的约束修改辐射场的方法，但修改仅限于颜色编辑或对象平移和旋转。在本文中，我们提出了一种方法，允许用户对场景的隐式表示进行可控的形状变形，并合成编辑场景的新颖视图图像，而无需重新训练网络。具体来说，我们在提取的显式网格表示和目标场景的隐式神经表示之间建立对应关系。用户可以首先利用成熟的基于网格的变形方法来使场景的网格表示变形。然后，我们的方法利用用户对网格表示的编辑，通过引入四面体网格作为代理来弯曲相机光线，从而获得编辑场景的渲染结果。大量的实验表明，我们的框架不仅可以在合成数据上实现理想的编辑结果，而且可以在用户捕获的真实场景上实现理想的编辑结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需重新训练网络即可对神经辐射场（NeRF）场景进行可控几何变形的方法。
AI.motivation: 现有NeRF方法无法实现用户控制的场景形状变形，仅支持颜色编辑或物体平移旋转等有限操作。
AI.method: 通过建立显式网格表示与隐式神经表示的对应关系，利用网格变形方法修改场景网格，并借助四面体网格代理弯曲相机光线实现编辑后场景渲染。
AI.result: 在合成数据和用户采集的真实场景中均取得理想的编辑效果。
AI.conclusion: 该框架有效实现了NeRF场景的可控几何编辑，拓展了神经渲染的应用范围。

title: The Future of Hybrid Meetings
title_zh: 混合会议的未来
comment: 10 pages, 1 figure
comments: 10 pages, 1 figure
abstract: Meetings are typically considered to be the fuel of an organization's productivity -- a place where employees discuss ideas and make collective decisions. However, it is no secret that meetings are also often perceived as wasteful vacuums, depleting employee morale and productivity, likely due to the fact that current technologies fall short in fully supporting physical or virtual meeting experience. In this position paper, we discuss the three key elements that make a meeting successful (i.e., execution, psychological safety, and physical comfort), and present new tools for hybrid meetings that incorporate those elements. As past research has focused on supporting meeting execution (the first element), we set the roadmap for future research on the two other elements: on psychological safety by articulating how new technologies could make meeting useful for all participants, ensure all participants give and receive appropriate levels of attention, and enable all participants to feel and make others feel comfortable; and on physical comfort by dwelling on how new technologies could make the meeting experience comfortable by integrating all human senses. We also discuss the potential danger of these technologies inadvertently becoming surveillance tools.
summary: Meetings are typically considered to be the fuel of an organization's productivity -- a place where employees discuss ideas and make collective decisions. However, it is no secret that meetings are also often perceived as wasteful vacuums, depleting employee morale and productivity, likely due to the fact that current technologies fall short in fully supporting physical or virtual meeting experience. In this position paper, we discuss the three key elements that make a meeting successful (i.e., execution, psychological safety, and physical comfort), and present new tools for hybrid meetings that incorporate those elements. As past research has focused on supporting meeting execution (the first element), we set the roadmap for future research on the two other elements: on psychological safety by articulating how new technologies could make meeting useful for all participants, ensure all participants give and receive appropriate levels of attention, and enable all participants to feel and make others feel comfortable; and on physical comfort by dwelling on how new technologies could make the meeting experience comfortable by integrating all human senses. We also discuss the potential danger of these technologies inadvertently becoming surveillance tools.
abstract_zh: 会议通常被认为是组织生产力的燃料——员工在这里讨论想法并做出集体决策。然而，众所周知，会议也常常被认为是浪费资源，会削弱员工的士气和生产力，这可能是因为当前的技术无法完全支持物理或虚拟会议体验。在这份立场文件中，我们讨论了会议成功的三个关键要素（即执行、心理安全和身体舒适），并提出了融合这些要素的混合会议的新工具。由于过去的研究重点是支持会议执行（第一个要素），我们为未来的研究制定了另外两个要素的路线图：心理安全，阐明新技术如何使会议对所有参与者都有用，确保所有参与者给予和接受适当程度的关注，并使所有参与者感到舒适并让其他人感到舒适；以及身体舒适度，深入探讨新技术如何通过整合所有人类感官来使会议体验变得舒适。我们还讨论了这些技术无意中成为监视工具的潜在危险。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨混合会议的成功关键要素，提出新工具并规划未来研究方向，同时警示技术潜在风险。
AI.motivation: 当前技术无法有效支持线上线下会议体验，导致效率低下，需改进会议质量。
AI.method: 分析现有研究，提出成功要素，并规划未来技术研究方向。
AI.result: 提出新工具和未来研究路线图，讨论技术可能带来的监控风险。
AI.conclusion: 未来混合会议技术需兼顾心理安全、物理舒适，平衡效率与隐私保护。

In this position paper, we discuss the three key elements that make a meeting successful (i.e., execution, psychological safety, and physical comfort), and present new tools for hybrid meetings that incorporate those elements. As past research has focused on supporting meeting execution (the first element), we set the roadmap for future research on the two other elements: on psychological safety by articulating how new technologies could make meeting useful for all participants, ensure all participants give and receive appropriate levels of attention, and enable all participants to feel and make others feel comfortable; and on physical comfort by dwelling on how new technologies could make the meeting experience comfortable by integrating all human senses. We also discuss the potential danger of these technologies inadvertently becoming surveillance tools. 会议通常被认为是组织生产力的燃料——员工在这里讨论想法并做出集体决策。然而，众所周知，会议也常常被认为是浪费资源，会削弱员工的士气和生产力，这可能是因为当前的技术无法完全支持物理或虚拟会议体验。在这份立场文件中，我们讨论了会议成功的三个关键要素（即执行、心理安全和身体舒适），并提出了融合这些要素的混合会议的新工具。由于过去的研究重点是支持会议执行（第一个要素），我们为未来的研究制定了另外两个要素的路线图：心理安全，阐明新技术如何使会议对所有参与者都有用，确保所有参与者给予和接受适当程度的关注，并使所有参与者感到舒适并让其他人感到舒适；以及身体舒适度，深入探讨新技术如何通过整合所有人类感官来使会议体验变得舒适。我们还讨论了这些技术无意中成为监视工具的潜在危险。 本文探讨混合会议的成功关键要素，提出新工具并规划未来研究方向，同时警示技术潜在风险。 Marios Constantinides,Daniele Quercia cs.HC

title: How Game Jams and Hackathons Accelerate Design Processes
title_zh: 游戏 Jam 和黑客马拉松如何加速设计流程
comment: PhD thesis, handed in 12th November 2020, defended 8th March 2021 at Aarhus University, Denmark. Please cite as: Falk, Jeanette (2021). How Game Jams and Hackathons Accelerate Design Processes. PhD thesis. Aarhus University. doi: https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2205.04966
comments: PhD thesis, handed in 12th November 2020, defended 8th March 2021 at Aarhus University, Denmark. Please cite as: Falk, Jeanette (2021). How Game Jams and Hackathons Accelerate Design Processes. PhD thesis. Aarhus University. doi: https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2205.04966
abstract: This dissertation presents three years of research on how design processes in game jams and hackathons can be understood as accelerated. Hackathons and game jams can both be described as formats where participants engage in designing and developing prototypes during an intentionally short time frame, such as 48 hours, which is meant to facilitate creativity, and encourage fast decision making and rapid prototyping. Game jams and hackathons are organised in many different contexts and for many different purposes as well, such as: internally in companies to spark new ideas; for fostering citizen innovation for municipalities; in cultural and governmental agencies; integral parts of education; entry points for developers wanting to enter especially the game industry (Olesen, 2020; Kultima, 2015). During the recent decade, game jams and hackathons have been introduced to academia as well, as formats for teaching and learning, and as research platforms as well. Only few research contributions engage with understanding how accelerated design processes in game jams and hackathons unfold, or how the organisation of game jam and hackathon formats influence these accelerated design processes. The main contributions of my PhD project are: 1) Descriptive process-level knowledge, which contextualise and solidify how accelerated design processes unfold under the circumstances of a game jam and a hackathon. 2) Overviews of how game jams have been organised for supporting participants' creativity and of how hackathons have been used as means and as research focus within academia. 3) Exploring how game jam and hackathon formats may be organised in order to support knowledge generation such as within academia, and in order to support creativity.

Hackathons and game jams can both be described as formats where participants engage in designing and developing prototypes during an intentionally short time frame, such as 48 hours, which is meant to facilitate creativity, and encourage fast decision making and rapid prototyping. Game jams and hackathons are organised in many different contexts and for many different purposes as well, such as: internally in companies to spark new ideas; for fostering citizen innovation for municipalities; in cultural and governmental agencies; integral parts of education; entry points for developers wanting to enter especially the game industry (Olesen, 2020; Kultima, 2015). During the recent decade, game jams and hackathons have been introduced to academia as well, as formats for teaching and learning, and as research platforms as well. Only few research contributions engage with understanding how accelerated design processes in game jams and hackathons unfold, or how the organisation of game jam and hackathon formats influence these accelerated design processes. The main contributions of my PhD project are: 1) Descriptive process-level knowledge, which contextualise and solidify how accelerated design processes unfold under the circumstances of a game jam and a hackathon. 2) Overviews of how game jams have been organised for supporting participants' creativity and of how hackathons have been used as means and as research focus within academia. 3) Exploring how game jam and hackathon formats may be organised in order to support knowledge generation such as within academia, and in order to support creativity.
abstract_zh: 本论文介绍了三年来关于如何理解游戏即兴和黑客马拉松中的设计过程加速的研究。黑客马拉松和游戏即兴活动都可以被描述为参与者在有意的短时间内（例如 48 小时）参与设计和开发原型的形式，这旨在促进创造力，并鼓励快速决策和快速原型制作。游戏即兴活动和黑客马拉松是在许多不同的背景下组织的，也有许多不同的目的，例如：在公司内部激发新想法；促进市政当局的公民创新；在文化和政府机构；教育的组成部分；想要进入游戏行业的开发者的切入点（Olesen，2020；Kultima，2015）。近十年来，游戏大赛和黑客马拉松也被引入学术界，作为教学和学习的形式以及研究平台。只有很少的研究贡献涉及了解游戏即兴和黑客马拉松中的加速设计过程如何展开，或者游戏即兴和黑客马拉松形式的组织如何影响这些加速设计过程。我的博士项目的主要贡献是：1）描述性流程级知识，它将加速设计流程在游戏果酱和黑客马拉松的情况下如何展开进行情境化和巩固。 2) 概述如何组织游戏即兴活动来支持参与者的创造力，以及如何将黑客马拉松用作学术界的手段和研究重点。 3) 探索如何组织游戏即兴活动和黑客马拉松形式，以支持学术界等知识生成，并支持创造力。

AI.tldr: 本文研究游戏马拉松和黑客松如何通过其组织形式加速设计流程，并探讨其对创造力和知识生成的影响。
AI.motivation: 尽管游戏马拉松和黑客松被广泛用于激发创新，但其加速设计流程的具体机制及组织形式的影响尚未被充分研究。
AI.method: 通过三年的实证研究，结合案例分析、文献综述及对现有学术研究的整合（如Olesen, 2020; Kultima, 2015），描述加速设计过程的特征并分析其组织模式。
AI.result: 提出三方面贡献：1) 描述加速设计流程的场景化知识；2) 总结游戏马拉松与黑客松在支持创造力和学术研究中的组织方式；3) 探索如何优化这些活动以促进知识生成与创造力。
AI.conclusion: 游戏马拉松和黑客松的组织形式需根据目标（如教育、产业创新）进行调整，以最大化其对创造力和知识生产的促进作用。

title: Parallel Batch-Dynamic Minimum Spanning Forest and the Efficiency of Dynamic Agglomerative Graph Clustering
title_zh: 并行批量动态最小生成森林和动态凝聚图聚类的效率
comment: SPAA 2022
comments: SPAA 2022
abstract: Hierarchical agglomerative clustering (HAC) is a popular algorithm for clustering data, but despite its importance, no dynamic algorithms for HAC with good theoretical guarantees exist. In this paper, we study dynamic HAC on edge-weighted graphs. As single-linkage HAC reduces to computing a minimum spanning forest (MSF), our first result is a parallel batch-dynamic algorithm for maintaining MSFs. On a batch of $k$ edge insertions or deletions, our batch-dynamic MSF algorithm runs in $O(k\log^6 n)$ expected amortized work and $O(\log^4 n)$ span with high probability. It is the first fully dynamic MSF algorithm handling batches of edge updates with polylogarithmic work per update and polylogarithmic span. Using our MSF algorithm, we obtain a parallel batch-dynamic algorithm that can answer queries about single-linkage graph HAC clusters. Our second result is that dynamic graph HAC is significantly harder for other common linkage functions. For example, assuming the strong exponential time hypothesis, dynamic graph HAC requires $Ω(n^{1-o(1)})$ work per update or query on a graph with $n$ vertices for complete linkage, weighted average linkage, and average linkage. For complete linkage and weighted average linkage, the bound still holds even for incremental or decremental algorithms and even if we allow $\operatorname{poly}(n)$-approximation. For average linkage, the bound weakens to $Ω(n^{1/2 - o(1)})$ for incremental and decremental algorithms, and the bounds still hold when allowing $n^{o(1)}$-approximation.
summary: Hierarchical agglomerative clustering (HAC) is a popular algorithm for clustering data, but despite its importance, no dynamic algorithms for HAC with good theoretical guarantees exist. In this paper, we study dynamic HAC on edge-weighted graphs. As single-linkage HAC reduces to computing a minimum spanning forest (MSF), our first result is a parallel batch-dynamic algorithm for maintaining MSFs. On a batch of $k$ edge insertions or deletions, our batch-dynamic MSF algorithm runs in $O(k\log^6 n)$ expected amortized work and $O(\log^4 n)$ span with high probability. It is the first fully dynamic MSF algorithm handling batches of edge updates with polylogarithmic work per update and polylogarithmic span. Using our MSF algorithm, we obtain a parallel batch-dynamic algorithm that can answer queries about single-linkage graph HAC clusters. Our second result is that dynamic graph HAC is significantly harder for other common linkage functions. For example, assuming the strong exponential time hypothesis, dynamic graph HAC requires $Ω(n^{1-o(1)})$ work per update or query on a graph with $n$ vertices for complete linkage, weighted average linkage, and average linkage. For complete linkage and weighted average linkage, the bound still holds even for incremental or decremental algorithms and even if we allow $\operatorname{poly}(n)$-approximation. For average linkage, the bound weakens to $Ω(n^{1/2 - o(1)})$ for incremental and decremental algorithms, and the bounds still hold when allowing $n^{o(1)}$-approximation.
abstract_zh: 层次凝聚聚类（HAC）是一种流行的数据聚类算法，但尽管它很重要，但尚不存在具有良好理论保证的HAC动态算法。在本文中，我们研究了边加权图上的动态 HAC。由于单链接 HAC 简化为计算最小生成森林 (MSF)，我们的第一个结果是用于维护 MSF 的并行批量动态算法。在一批 $k$ 边缘插入或删除中，我们的批量动态 MSF 算法以 $O(k\log^6 n)$ 预期摊销工作和 $O(\log^4 n)$ 高概率运行。它是第一个完全动态的 MSF 算法，通过每次更新的多对数工作和多对数跨度来处理批量边缘更新。使用我们的 MSF 算法，我们获得了一种并行批动态算法，可以回答有关单链接图 HAC 集群的查询。我们的第二个结果是，动态图 HAC 对于其他常见的链接功能来说要困难得多。例如，假设强指数时间假设，动态图 HAC 需要对具有 $n$ 个顶点的图进行每次更新或查询 $Ω(n^{1-o(1)})$ 工作，以实现完全链接、加权平均链接和平均链接。对于完整链接和加权平均链接，即使对于增量或减量算法，并且即使我们允许 $\operatorname{poly}(n)$ 近似，界限仍然成立。对于平均链接，增量和减量算法的界限减弱为 $Ω(n^{1/2 - o(1)})$，并且当允许 $n^{o(1)}$ 近似时，界限仍然成立。

AI.tldr: 本文研究动态图的层次聚类算法，提出并行批量动态最小生成森林算法，并分析不同链接函数下动态图聚类的复杂度。
AI.motivation: 层次聚合聚类（HAC）是重要算法，但缺乏具有理论保证的动态算法。单链接HAC等价于最小生成森林（MSF）问题，而其他链接函数的动态HAC复杂度尚不明确。
AI.method: 设计并行批量动态MSF算法处理边插入/删除，结合复杂度理论假设（如强指数时间假设）分析其他链接函数的动态HAC下界。
AI.result: 首次提出全动态MSF算法，单次更新工作量和跨度均为多项式对数；证明其他链接函数的动态HAC需近线性工作量，增量/减量算法亦无法突破下界。
AI.conclusion: 单链接HAC可通过高效动态MSF算法处理，但其他常见链接函数的动态HAC在理论下界上显著困难，需依赖近似方法或不同技术路线。

motivation: 层次聚合聚类（HAC）是重要算法，但缺乏具有理论保证的动态算法。单链接HAC等价于最小生成森林（MSF）问题，而其他链接函数的动态HAC复杂度尚不明确。
method: 设计并行批量动态MSF算法处理边插入/删除，结合复杂度理论假设（如强指数时间假设）分析其他链接函数的动态HAC下界。
result: 首次提出全动态MSF算法，单次更新工作量和跨度均为多项式对数；证明其他链接函数的动态HAC需近线性工作量，增量/减量算法亦无法突破下界。
conclusion: 单链接HAC可通过高效动态MSF算法处理，但其他常见链接函数的动态HAC在理论下界上显著困难，需依赖近似方法或不同技术路线。
search_text: Parallel Batch-Dynamic Minimum Spanning Forest and the Efficiency of Dynamic Agglomerative Graph Clustering 并行批量动态最小生成森林和动态凝聚图聚类的效率 Hierarchical agglomerative clustering (HAC) is a popular algorithm for clustering data, but despite its importance, no dynamic algorithms for HAC with good theoretical guarantees exist. In this paper, we study dynamic HAC on edge-weighted graphs. As single-linkage HAC reduces to computing a minimum spanning forest (MSF), our first result is a parallel batch-dynamic algorithm for maintaining MSFs. On a batch of $k$ edge insertions or deletions, our batch-dynamic MSF algorithm runs in $O(k\log^6 n)$ expected amortized work and $O(\log^4 n)$ span with high probability. It is the first fully dynamic MSF algorithm handling batches of edge updates with polylogarithmic work per update and polylogarithmic span. Using our MSF algorithm, we obtain a parallel batch-dynamic algorithm that can answer queries about single-linkage graph HAC clusters. Our second result is that dynamic graph HAC is significantly harder for other common linkage functions. For example, assuming the strong exponential time hypothesis, dynamic graph HAC requires $Ω(n^{1-o(1)})$ work per update or query on a graph with $n$ vertices for complete linkage, weighted average linkage, and average linkage. For complete linkage and weighted average linkage, the bound still holds even for incremental or decremental algorithms and even if we allow $\operatorname{poly}(n)$-approximation. For average linkage, the bound weakens to $Ω(n^{1/2 - o(1)})$ for incremental and decremental algorithms, and the bounds still hold when allowing $n^{o(1)}$-approximation. 层次凝聚聚类（HAC）是一种流行的数据聚类算法，但尽管它很重要，但尚不存在具有良好理论保证的HAC动态算法。在本文中，我们研究了边加权图上的动态 HAC。由于单链接 HAC 简化为计算最小生成森林 (MSF)，我们的第一个结果是用于维护 MSF 的并行批量动态算法。在一批 $k$ 边缘插入或删除中，我们的批量动态 MSF 算法以 $O(k\log^6 n)$ 预期摊销工作和 $O(\log^4 n)$ 高概率运行。它是第一个完全动态的 MSF 算法，通过每次更新的多对数工作和多对数跨度来处理批量边缘更新。使用我们的 MSF 算法，我们获得了一种并行批动态算法，可以回答有关单链接图 HAC 集群的查询。我们的第二个结果是，动态图 HAC 对于其他常见的链接功能来说要困难得多。例如，假设强指数时间假设，动态图 HAC 需要对具有 $n$ 个顶点的图进行每次更新或查询 $Ω(n^{1-o(1)})$ 工作，以实现完全链接、加权平均链接和平均链接。对于完整链接和加权平均链接，即使对于增量或减量算法，并且即使我们允许 $\operatorname{poly}(n)$ 近似，界限仍然成立。对于平均链接，增量和减量算法的界限减弱为 $Ω(n^{1/2 - o(1)})$，并且当允许 $n^{o(1)}$ 近似时，界限仍然成立。 本文研究动态图的层次聚类算法，提出并行批量动态最小生成森林算法，并分析不同链接函数下动态图聚类的复杂度。 Tom Tseng,Laxman Dhulipala,Julian Shun cs.DS,cs.CC,cs.DC

title: Read the Room: Adapting a Robot's Voice to Ambient and Social Contexts
title_zh: 阅读房间：使机器人的声音适应环境和社会环境
comment: 8 pages, 10 figures, Accepted to International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems, project webpage: https://rosielab.github.io/ambiance/
comments: 8 pages, 10 figures, Accepted to International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems, project webpage: https://rosielab.github.io/ambiance/
abstract: How should a robot speak in a formal, quiet and dark, or a bright, lively and noisy environment? By designing robots to speak in a more social and ambient-appropriate manner we can improve perceived awareness and intelligence for these agents. We describe a process and results toward selecting robot voice styles for perceived social appropriateness and ambiance awareness. Understanding how humans adapt their voices in different acoustic settings can be challenging due to difficulties in voice capture in the wild. Our approach includes 3 steps: (a) Collecting and validating voice data interactions in virtual Zoom ambiances, (b) Exploration and clustering human vocal utterances to identify primary voice styles, and (c) Testing robot voice styles in recreated ambiances using projections, lighting and sound. We focus on food service scenarios as a proof-of-concept setting. We provide results using the Pepper robot's voice with different styles, towards robots that speak in a contextually appropriate and adaptive manner. Our results with N=120 participants provide evidence that the choice of voice style in different ambiances impacted a robot's perceived intelligence in several factors including: social appropriateness, comfort, awareness, human-likeness and competency.
summary: How should a robot speak in a formal, quiet and dark, or a bright, lively and noisy environment? By designing robots to speak in a more social and ambient-appropriate manner we can improve perceived awareness and intelligence for these agents. We describe a process and results toward selecting robot voice styles for perceived social appropriateness and ambiance awareness. Understanding how humans adapt their voices in different acoustic settings can be challenging due to difficulties in voice capture in the wild. Our approach includes 3 steps: (a) Collecting and validating voice data interactions in virtual Zoom ambiances, (b) Exploration and clustering human vocal utterances to identify primary voice styles, and (c) Testing robot voice styles in recreated ambiances using projections, lighting and sound. We focus on food service scenarios as a proof-of-concept setting. We provide results using the Pepper robot's voice with different styles, towards robots that speak in a contextually appropriate and adaptive manner. Our results with N=120 participants provide evidence that the choice of voice style in different ambiances impacted a robot's perceived intelligence in several factors including: social appropriateness, comfort, awareness, human-likeness and competency.
abstract_zh: 机器人在正式、安静、黑暗，还是明亮、热闹、嘈杂的环境中应该如何说话？通过设计机器人以更加社交和适合环境的方式说话，我们可以提高这些代理的感知意识和智力。我们描述了选择机器人语音风格以感知社交适当性和氛围意识的过程和结果。由于野外语音捕捉的困难，了解人类如何在不同的声学环境中调整自己的声音可能具有挑战性。我们的方法包括 3 个步骤：(a) 在虚拟 Zoom 环境中收集和验证语音数据交互，(b) 探索和聚类人类语音以识别主要语音风格，以及 (c) 使用投影、灯光和声音在重建的环境中测试机器人语音风格。我们专注于餐饮服务场景作为概念验证设置。我们使用 Pepper 机器人的不同风格的声音提供结果，以针对以上下文适当和自适应方式说话的机器人。我们对 N=120 名参与者的结果证明，不同环境下语音风格的选择会在多个因素上影响机器人的感知智力，包括：社交适宜性、舒适度、意识、人类相似性和能力。

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何根据环境和社会情境调整机器人语音，以提升其感知的智能性和适应性。
AI.motivation: 机器人需在不同场景（如安静或嘈杂环境）中自然互动，但现有语音策略缺乏对环境和社会语境的适应性，影响用户体验。
AI.method: 通过三步骤：1) 在虚拟Zoom环境中收集和验证语音数据；2) 聚类人类语音以识别主要语音风格；3) 在模拟环境中测试Pepper机器人不同语音风格的效果。
AI.result: 120名参与者实验表明，不同语音风格显著影响机器人感知的智能性、社交适当性、舒适度和人类相似性。
AI.conclusion: 根据环境动态调整机器人语音风格可提升其社会适应性，为服务机器人提供可行的交互优化方案。

title: Transformer-based Cross-Modal Recipe Embeddings with Large Batch Training
title_zh: 基于 Transformer 的跨模式配方嵌入与大批量训练
comment: Accepted at MMM2023
comments: Accepted at MMM2023
abstract: In this paper, we present a cross-modal recipe retrieval framework, Transformer-based Network for Large Batch Training (TNLBT), which is inspired by ACME~(Adversarial Cross-Modal Embedding) and H-T~(Hierarchical Transformer). TNLBT aims to accomplish retrieval tasks while generating images from recipe embeddings. We apply the Hierarchical Transformer-based recipe text encoder, the Vision Transformer~(ViT)-based recipe image encoder, and an adversarial network architecture to enable better cross-modal embedding learning for recipe texts and images. In addition, we use self-supervised learning to exploit the rich information in the recipe texts having no corresponding images. Since contrastive learning could benefit from a larger batch size according to the recent literature on self-supervised learning, we adopt a large batch size during training and have validated its effectiveness. In the experiments, the proposed framework significantly outperformed the current state-of-the-art frameworks in both cross-modal recipe retrieval and image generation tasks on the benchmark Recipe1M. This is the first work which confirmed the effectiveness of large batch training on cross-modal recipe embeddings.
summary: In this paper, we present a cross-modal recipe retrieval framework, Transformer-based Network for Large Batch Training (TNLBT), which is inspired by ACME~(Adversarial Cross-Modal Embedding) and H-T~(Hierarchical Transformer). TNLBT aims to accomplish retrieval tasks while generating images from recipe embeddings. We apply the Hierarchical Transformer-based recipe text encoder, the Vision Transformer~(ViT)-based recipe image encoder, and an adversarial network architecture to enable better cross-modal embedding learning for recipe texts and images. In addition, we use self-supervised learning to exploit the rich information in the recipe texts having no corresponding images. Since contrastive learning could benefit from a larger batch size according to the recent literature on self-supervised learning, we adopt a large batch size during training and have validated its effectiveness. In the experiments, the proposed framework significantly outperformed the current state-of-the-art frameworks in both cross-modal recipe retrieval and image generation tasks on the benchmark Recipe1M. This is the first work which confirmed the effectiveness of large batch training on cross-modal recipe embeddings.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种跨模态食谱检索框架，即基于 Transformer 的大批量训练网络（TNLBT），该框架受到 ACME~（对抗性跨模态嵌入）和 H-T~（分层变压器）的启发。 TNLBT 旨在完成检索任务，同时从菜谱嵌入生成图像。我们应用基于 Hierarchical Transformer 的菜谱文本编码器、基于 Vision Transformer~(ViT) 的菜谱图像编码器和对抗网络架构，以实现更好的菜谱文本和图像的跨模态嵌入学习。此外，我们使用自我监督学习来利用没有相应图像的菜谱文本中的丰富信息。根据最近有关自监督学习的文献，对比学习可以受益于较大的批量大小，因此我们在训练过程中采用了较大的批量大小并验证了其有效性。在实验中，所提出的框架在基准 Recipe1M 上的跨模式菜谱检索和图像生成任务中显着优于当前最先进的框架。这是第一个证实跨模式配方嵌入大批量训练有效性的工作。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Transformer的跨模态食谱嵌入框架TNLBT，通过大批次训练实现食谱文本与图像的交叉检索及图像生成。
AI.motivation: 现有跨模态食谱检索方法在处理文本与图像对齐时存在局限，且未充分验证大批次训练对嵌入学习的效果。
AI.method: 结合层次化Transformer文本编码器、ViT图像编码器与对抗网络，利用自监督学习处理无图像文本，并采用大批次训练优化对比学习。
AI.result: 在Recipe1M基准测试中，TNLBT在跨模态检索和图像生成任务上均显著优于现有最优方法，首次验证大批次训练的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 大批次训练可有效提升跨模态食谱嵌入性能，未来可进一步探索架构优化与多模态对齐策略。

This is the first work which confirmed the effectiveness of large batch training on cross-modal recipe embeddings. 在本文中，我们提出了一种跨模态食谱检索框架，即基于 Transformer 的大批量训练网络（TNLBT），该框架受到 ACME~（对抗性跨模态嵌入）和 H-T~（分层变压器）的启发。 TNLBT 旨在完成检索任务，同时从菜谱嵌入生成图像。我们应用基于 Hierarchical Transformer 的菜谱文本编码器、基于 Vision Transformer~(ViT) 的菜谱图像编码器和对抗网络架构，以实现更好的菜谱文本和图像的跨模态嵌入学习。此外，我们使用自我监督学习来利用没有相应图像的菜谱文本中的丰富信息。根据最近有关自监督学习的文献，对比学习可以受益于较大的批量大小，因此我们在训练过程中采用了较大的批量大小并验证了其有效性。在实验中，所提出的框架在基准 Recipe1M 上的跨模式菜谱检索和图像生成任务中显着优于当前最先进的框架。这是第一个证实跨模式配方嵌入大批量训练有效性的工作。 本文提出一种基于Transformer的跨模态食谱嵌入框架TNLBT，通过大批次训练实现食谱文本与图像的交叉检索及图像生成。 Jing Yang,Junwen Chen,Keiji Yanai cs.CV

authors_text: Lukas Gianinazzi,Tal Ben-Nun,Maciej Besta,Saleh Ashkboos,Yves Baumann,Piotr Luczynski,Torsten Hoefler
title: The spatial computer: A model for energy-efficient parallel computation
title_zh: 空间计算机：节能并行计算模型
abstract: We present a new parallel model of computation suitable for spatial architectures, for which the energy used for communication heavily depends on the distance of the communicating processors. In our model, processors have locations on a conceptual two-dimensional grid, and their distance therein determines their communication cost. In particular, we introduce the energy cost of a spatial computation, which measures the total distance traveled by all messages, and study the depth of communication, which measures the largest number of hops of a chain of messages. We show matching energy lower- and upper bounds for many foundational problems, including sorting, median selection, and matrix multiplication. Our model does not depend on any parameters other than the input shape and size, simplifying algorithm analysis. We also show how to simulate PRAM algorithms in our model and how to obtain results for a more complex model that introduces the size of the local memories of the processors as a parameter.
summary: We present a new parallel model of computation suitable for spatial architectures, for which the energy used for communication heavily depends on the distance of the communicating processors. In our model, processors have locations on a conceptual two-dimensional grid, and their distance therein determines their communication cost. In particular, we introduce the energy cost of a spatial computation, which measures the total distance traveled by all messages, and study the depth of communication, which measures the largest number of hops of a chain of messages. We show matching energy lower- and upper bounds for many foundational problems, including sorting, median selection, and matrix multiplication. Our model does not depend on any parameters other than the input shape and size, simplifying algorithm analysis. We also show how to simulate PRAM algorithms in our model and how to obtain results for a more complex model that introduces the size of the local memories of the processors as a parameter.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种适用于空间架构的新并行计算模型，其中用于通信的能量在很大程度上取决于通信处理器的距离。在我们的模型中，处理器在概念性二维网格上具有位置，它们在其中的距离决定了它们的通信成本。特别是，我们引入了空间计算的能量成本，它测量所有消息传播的总距离，并研究通信深度，它测量消息链的最大跳数。我们展示了许多基础问题的匹配能量下限和上限，包括排序、中值选择和矩阵乘法。我们的模型不依赖于输入形状和大小以外的任何参数，从而简化了算法分析。我们还展示了如何在模型中模拟 PRAM 算法，以及如何获得更复杂模型的结果，该模型引入处理器本地存储器的大小作为参数。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种适用于空间架构的并行计算模型，通过考虑处理器间通信距离对能耗的影响，建立能量成本和通信深度的度量体系，并分析多个基础问题的能效上下界。
AI.motivation: 传统并行计算模型未充分考虑空间架构中通信距离对能耗的直接影响，而实际计算中通信成本与距离密切相关，需建立更贴近物理特性的能效分析框架。
AI.method: 在二维网格上定义处理器位置，以消息传输总距离作为能量成本，通信深度为消息链最大跳数，通过理论分析建立多个基础问题的能效上下界，并探讨PRAM算法模拟及内存参数扩展。
AI.result: 为排序、中位数选择和矩阵乘法等基础问题建立匹配的能效上下界，模型仅依赖输入规模参数简化分析，同时实现PRAM算法模拟及内存参数扩展方法。
AI.conclusion: 该模型为能效并行计算提供新的理论框架，可指导节能算法设计，并通过参数扩展适应更复杂的计算场景。

title: Fast Obstacle Avoidance Based on Real-Time Sensing
title_zh: 基于实时感知的快速避障
abstract: Humans are remarkable at navigating and moving through dynamic and complex spaces, such as crowded streets. For robots to do the same, it is crucial that they are endowed with highly reactive obstacle avoidance robust to partial and poor sensing. We address the issue of enabling obstacle avoidance based on sparse and asynchronous perception. The proposed control scheme combines a high-level input command provided by either a planner or a human operator with fast reactive obstacle avoidance. The sampling-based sensor data can be combined with an analytical reconstruction of the obstacles for real-time collision avoidance. We can ensure that the agent does not get stuck when a feasible path exists between obstacles. The algorithm was evaluated experimentally on static laser data from cluttered, indoor office environments. Additionally, it was used in a shared control mode in a dynamic and complex outdoor environment in the center of Lausanne. The proposed control scheme successfully avoided collisions in both scenarios. During the experiments, the controller on the onboard computer took 1 millisecond to evaluate over 30000 data points.
summary: Humans are remarkable at navigating and moving through dynamic and complex spaces, such as crowded streets. For robots to do the same, it is crucial that they are endowed with highly reactive obstacle avoidance robust to partial and poor sensing. We address the issue of enabling obstacle avoidance based on sparse and asynchronous perception. The proposed control scheme combines a high-level input command provided by either a planner or a human operator with fast reactive obstacle avoidance. The sampling-based sensor data can be combined with an analytical reconstruction of the obstacles for real-time collision avoidance. We can ensure that the agent does not get stuck when a feasible path exists between obstacles. The algorithm was evaluated experimentally on static laser data from cluttered, indoor office environments. Additionally, it was used in a shared control mode in a dynamic and complex outdoor environment in the center of Lausanne. The proposed control scheme successfully avoided collisions in both scenarios. During the experiments, the controller on the onboard computer took 1 millisecond to evaluate over 30000 data points.
abstract_zh: 人类非常擅长在动态和复杂的空间（例如拥挤的街道）中导航和移动。对于机器人来说，要做到同样的事情，至关重要的是赋予它们高反应性的避障能力，对部分和不良传感具有鲁棒性。我们解决了基于稀疏和异步感知的避障问题。所提出的控制方案将规划者或操作人员提供的高级输入命令与快速反应避障相结合。基于采样的传感器数据可以与障碍物的分析重建相结合，以实现实时避免碰撞。当障碍物之间存在可行路径时，我们可以确保代理不会被卡住。该算法在杂乱的室内办公环境中的静态激光数据上进行了实验评估。此外，它还在洛桑市中心动态复杂的室外环境中以共享控制模式使用。所提出的控制方案成功地避免了两种情况下的碰撞。在实验过程中，机载计算机上的控制器花了 1 毫秒的时间评估了 30000 多个数据点。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于实时感知的快速避障控制方案，结合高层指令与反应式避障，以应对动态复杂环境中的稀疏感知挑战。
AI.motivation: 人类能高效导航复杂动态空间，但机器人需具备在部分可观测环境下鲁棒的避障能力，尤其在感知数据稀疏或异步时。
AI.method: 融合高层规划指令与实时反应式避障，通过采样传感器数据与障碍物解析重建相结合，实现毫秒级碰撞规避计算。
AI.result: 算法在室内静态激光数据与户外动态复杂场景中均成功避障， onboard控制器处理3万数据点仅需1毫秒。
AI.conclusion: 该控制方案验证了稀疏感知下实时避障的可行性，为机器人动态环境导航提供了高效可靠的技术路径。

title: Gamified Speaker Comparison by Listening
title_zh: 通过聆听进行游戏化演讲者比较
comment: Accepted to Odyssey 2022 The Speaker and Language Recognition Workshop
comments: Accepted to Odyssey 2022 The Speaker and Language Recognition Workshop
abstract: We address speaker comparison by listening in a game-like environment, hypothesized to make the task more motivating for naive listeners. We present the same 30 trials selected with the help of an x-vector speaker recognition system from VoxCeleb to a total of 150 crowdworkers recruited through Amazon's Mechanical Turk. They are divided into cohorts of 50, each using one of three alternative interface designs: (i) a traditional (nongamified) design; (ii) a gamified design with feedback on decisions, along with points, game level indications, and possibility for interface customization; (iii) another gamified design with an additional constraint of maximum of 5 'lives' consumed by wrong answers. We analyze the impact of these interface designs to listener error rates (both misses and false alarms), probability calibration, time of quitting, along with survey questionnaire. The results indicate improved performance from (i) to (ii) and (iii), particularly in terms of balancing the two types of detection errors.
summary: We address speaker comparison by listening in a game-like environment, hypothesized to make the task more motivating for naive listeners. We present the same 30 trials selected with the help of an x-vector speaker recognition system from VoxCeleb to a total of 150 crowdworkers recruited through Amazon's Mechanical Turk. They are divided into cohorts of 50, each using one of three alternative interface designs: (i) a traditional (nongamified) design; (ii) a gamified design with feedback on decisions, along with points, game level indications, and possibility for interface customization; (iii) another gamified design with an additional constraint of maximum of 5 'lives' consumed by wrong answers. We analyze the impact of these interface designs to listener error rates (both misses and false alarms), probability calibration, time of quitting, along with survey questionnaire. The results indicate improved performance from (i) to (ii) and (iii), particularly in terms of balancing the two types of detection errors.
abstract_zh: 我们通过在类似游戏的环境中聆听来进行说话者比较，假设这样可以使这项任务对天真的听众更具激励性。我们向通过亚马逊 Mechanical Turk 招募的总共 150 名众包工作者展示了在 VoxCeleb 的 x-矢量说话人识别系统的帮助下选择的 30 项相同的试验。他们被分成 50 人一组，每组使用三种替代界面设计中的一种：（i）传统（非游戏化）设计； (ii) 游戏化设计，包含决策反馈、积分、游戏级别指示以及界面定制的可能性； (iii) 另一种游戏化设计，附加了最多 5 条“生命”被错误答案消耗的限制。我们分析了这些界面设计对听众错误率（漏报和误报）、概率校准、戒烟时间以及调查问卷的影响。结果表明，从 (i) 到 (ii) 和 (iii)，性能有所提高，特别是在平衡两种类型的检测误差方面。

AI.tldr: 本文通过游戏化界面设计提升非专业听众在说话人比较任务中的表现。
AI.motivation: 传统说话人比较任务枯燥且错误率高，游戏化设计可能提高参与度并优化错误平衡。
AI.method: 使用VoxCeleb数据集的30个试听任务，招募150名MTurk用户分组测试三种界面：传统设计、带反馈与定制化的游戏化设计、限制5次错误机会的游戏化设计，分析错误率、校准概率与退出时间。
AI.result: 游戏化设计（ii、iii）相较传统设计（i）显著降低错误率，尤其在平衡漏检与误报方面表现更优。
AI.conclusion: 游戏化元素（如反馈、定制化）能有效提升说话人比较任务的准确性与用户参与度。

search_text: Gamified Speaker Comparison by Listening 通过聆听进行游戏化演讲者比较 We address speaker comparison by listening in a game-like environment, hypothesized to make the task more motivating for naive listeners. We present the same 30 trials selected with the help of an x-vector speaker recognition system from VoxCeleb to a total of 150 crowdworkers recruited through Amazon's Mechanical Turk. They are divided into cohorts of 50, each using one of three alternative interface designs: (i) a traditional (nongamified) design; (ii) a gamified design with feedback on decisions, along with points, game level indications, and possibility for interface customization; (iii) another gamified design with an additional constraint of maximum of 5 'lives' consumed by wrong answers. We analyze the impact of these interface designs to listener error rates (both misses and false alarms), probability calibration, time of quitting, along with survey questionnaire. The results indicate improved performance from (i) to (ii) and (iii), particularly in terms of balancing the two types of detection errors. 我们通过在类似游戏的环境中聆听来进行说话者比较，假设这样可以使这项任务对天真的听众更具激励性。我们向通过亚马逊 Mechanical Turk 招募的总共 150 名众包工作者展示了在 VoxCeleb 的 x-矢量说话人识别系统的帮助下选择的 30 项相同的试验。他们被分成 50 人一组，每组使用三种替代界面设计中的一种：（i）传统（非游戏化）设计； (ii) 游戏化设计，包含决策反馈、积分、游戏级别指示以及界面定制的可能性； (iii) 另一种游戏化设计，附加了最多 5 条“生命”被错误答案消耗的限制。我们分析了这些界面设计对听众错误率（漏报和误报）、概率校准、戒烟时间以及调查问卷的影响。结果表明，从 (i) 到 (ii) 和 (iii)，性能有所提高，特别是在平衡两种类型的检测误差方面。 本文通过游戏化界面设计提升非专业听众在说话人比较任务中的表现。 Sandip Ghimire,Tomi Kinnunen,Rosa Gonzalez Hautamäki cs.SD,eess.AS

authors_text: Jonathan Zong,Crystal Lee,Alan Lundgard,JiWoong Jang,Daniel Hajas,Arvind Satyanarayan
title: Rich Screen Reader Experiences for Accessible Data Visualization
title_zh: 丰富的屏幕阅读器体验，实现可访问的数据可视化
comment: An accessible HTML version of this paper is available at: http://vis.csail.mit.edu/pubs/rich-screen-reader-vis-experiences
comments: An accessible HTML version of this paper is available at: http://vis.csail.mit.edu/pubs/rich-screen-reader-vis-experiences
abstract: Current web accessibility guidelines ask visualization designers to support screen readers via basic non-visual alternatives like textual descriptions and access to raw data tables. But charts do more than summarize data or reproduce tables; they afford interactive data exploration at varying levels of granularity -- from fine-grained datum-by-datum reading to skimming and surfacing high-level trends. In response to the lack of comparable non-visual affordances, we present a set of rich screen reader experiences for accessible data visualization and exploration. Through an iterative co-design process, we identify three key design dimensions for expressive screen reader accessibility: structure, or how chart entities should be organized for a screen reader to traverse; navigation, or the structural, spatial, and targeted operations a user might perform to step through the structure; and, description, or the semantic content, composition, and verbosity of the screen reader's narration. We operationalize these dimensions to prototype screen-reader-accessible visualizations that cover a diverse range of chart types and combinations of our design dimensions. We evaluate a subset of these prototypes in a mixed-methods study with 13 blind and low vision readers. Our findings demonstrate that these designs help users conceptualize data spatially, selectively attend to data of interest at different levels of granularity, and experience control and agency over their data analysis process. An accessible HTML version of this paper is available at: http://vis.csail.mit.edu/pubs/rich-screen-reader-vis-experiences.

But charts do more than summarize data or reproduce tables; they afford interactive data exploration at varying levels of granularity -- from fine-grained datum-by-datum reading to skimming and surfacing high-level trends. Through an iterative co-design process, we identify three key design dimensions for expressive screen reader accessibility: structure, or how chart entities should be organized for a screen reader to traverse; navigation, or the structural, spatial, and targeted operations a user might perform to step through the structure; and, description, or the semantic content, composition, and verbosity of the screen reader's narration. We operationalize these dimensions to prototype screen-reader-accessible visualizations that cover a diverse range of chart types and combinations of our design dimensions. We evaluate a subset of these prototypes in a mixed-methods study with 13 blind and low vision readers. Our findings demonstrate that these designs help users conceptualize data spatially, selectively attend to data of interest at different levels of granularity, and experience control and agency over their data analysis process. An accessible HTML version of this paper is available at: http://vis.csail.mit.edu/pubs/rich-screen-reader-vis-experiences.
abstract_zh: 当前的网络可访问性指南要求可视化设计人员通过基本的非视觉替代方案（例如文本描述和访问原始数据表）来支持屏幕阅读器。但图表的作用不仅仅是总结数据或重现表格；它们提供不同粒度级别的交互式数据探索——从细粒度的逐个数据读取到略读和呈现高级趋势。为了应对缺乏可比的非视觉可供性，我们提供了一组丰富的屏幕阅读器体验，用于可访问的数据可视化和探索。通过迭代协同设计过程，我们确定了富有表现力的屏幕阅读器可访问性的三个关键设计维度：结构，或如何组织图表实体以供屏幕阅读器遍历；导航，或用户为逐步浏览结构而可能执行的结构、空间和目标操作；以及屏幕阅读器叙述的描述或语义内容、组成和冗长程度。我们将这些维度操作为屏幕阅读器可访问的可视化原型，涵盖各种图表类型和我们设计维度的组合。我们在一项混合方法研究中对 13 名盲人和低视力读者的这些原型的子集进行了评估。我们的研究结果表明，这些设计可以帮助用户在空间上概念化数据，有选择地关注不同粒度级别的感兴趣数据，并体验对数据分析过程的控制和代理。本文的可访问 HTML 版本位于：http://vis.csail.mit.edu/pubs/rich-screen-reader-vis-experiences。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过设计丰富的屏幕阅读器体验，提升数据可视化的可访问性，使视障用户能够进行交互式数据探索。
AI.motivation: 当前网页无障碍指南仅要求提供文本描述和原始数据表，但图表的交互性探索功能（如粒度调整和趋势识别）缺乏对应的非视觉替代方案。
AI.method: 通过迭代共设计过程，提出结构、导航和描述三个设计维度，并开发覆盖多种图表类型的原型，结合13名盲人及低视力用户的混合方法研究进行评估。
AI.result: 用户能够更直观地理解数据空间关系，在不同粒度下选择性关注数据，并在分析过程中获得更高的控制感和自主性。
AI.conclusion: 通过结构化导航和语义化描述的屏幕阅读器设计，可有效增强数据可视化对视障用户的可访问性与交互性。

title: Reasoning in the Description Logic ALC under Category Semantics
title_zh: 类别语义下的描述逻辑 ALC 中的推理
comment: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2110.08837
comments: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2110.08837
abstract: We present in this paper a reformulation of the usual set-theoretical semantics of the description logic $\mathcal{ALC}$ with general TBoxes by using categorical language. In this setting, $\mathcal{ALC}$ concepts are represented as objects, concept subsumptions as arrows, and memberships as logical quantifiers over objects and arrows of categories. Such a category-based semantics provides a more modular representation of the semantics of $\mathcal{ALC}$. This feature allows us to define a sublogic of $\mathcal{ALC}$ by dropping the interaction between existential and universal restrictions, which would be responsible for an exponential complexity in space. Such a sublogic is undefinable in the usual set-theoretical semantics, We show that this sublogic is {\sc{PSPACE}} by proposing a deterministic algorithm for checking concept satisfiability which runs in polynomial space.
summary: We present in this paper a reformulation of the usual set-theoretical semantics of the description logic $\mathcal{ALC}$ with general TBoxes by using categorical language. In this setting, $\mathcal{ALC}$ concepts are represented as objects, concept subsumptions as arrows, and memberships as logical quantifiers over objects and arrows of categories. Such a category-based semantics provides a more modular representation of the semantics of $\mathcal{ALC}$. This feature allows us to define a sublogic of $\mathcal{ALC}$ by dropping the interaction between existential and universal restrictions, which would be responsible for an exponential complexity in space. Such a sublogic is undefinable in the usual set-theoretical semantics, We show that this sublogic is {\sc{PSPACE}} by proposing a deterministic algorithm for checking concept satisfiability which runs in polynomial space.
abstract_zh: 我们在本文中提出了使用分类语言用通用 TBox 重新表述描述逻辑 $\mathcal{ALC}$ 的常用集合论语义。在此设置中，$\mathcal{ALC}$ 概念表示为对象，概念包含表示为箭头，而成员资格表示为对象和类别箭头上的逻辑量词。这种基于类别的语义提供了 $\mathcal{ALC}$ 语义的更加模块化的表示。此功能允许我们通过删除存在限制和普遍限制之间的相互作用来定义 $\mathcal{ALC}$ 的子逻辑，这将导致空间的指数复杂性。这样的子逻辑在通常的集合论语义中是无法定义的。我们通过提出一种用于检查在多项式空间中运行的概念可满足性的确定性算法来证明该子逻辑是 {\sc{PSPACE}}。

AI.tldr: 本文将描述逻辑ALC的集合论语义转换为范畴语义，并基于此提出一种新的子逻辑以降低推理复杂度。
AI.motivation: 传统集合论语义难以有效处理ALC中存在与全称限制的交互导致的指数级空间复杂度，需探索更模块化的语义表示方法。
AI.method: 采用范畴论框架，将概念、子概念和成员关系映射为范畴中的对象、箭头及逻辑量词，通过消除存在与全称限制的交互定义子逻辑，并设计多项式空间算法验证概念可满足性。
AI.result: 成功构建了传统语义下不可定义的ALC子逻辑，证明其推理复杂度为PSPACE，并提出对应的确定性多项式空间算法。
AI.conclusion: 范畴语义为描述逻辑提供了更模块化的分析工具，有助于设计高效推理算法并揭示逻辑复杂度的本质特征。

ai_content.tldr: 本文将描述逻辑ALC的集合论语义转换为范畴语义，并基于此提出一种新的子逻辑以降低推理复杂度。
ai_content.motivation: 传统集合论语义难以有效处理ALC中存在与全称限制的交互导致的指数级空间复杂度，需探索更模块化的语义表示方法。
ai_content.method: 采用范畴论框架，将概念、子概念和成员关系映射为范畴中的对象、箭头及逻辑量词，通过消除存在与全称限制的交互定义子逻辑，并设计多项式空间算法验证概念可满足性。
ai_content.result: 成功构建了传统语义下不可定义的ALC子逻辑，证明其推理复杂度为PSPACE，并提出对应的确定性多项式空间算法。
ai_content.conclusion: 范畴语义为描述逻辑提供了更模块化的分析工具，有助于设计高效推理算法并揭示逻辑复杂度的本质特征。

search_text: Reasoning in the Description Logic ALC under Category Semantics 类别语义下的描述逻辑 ALC 中的推理 We present in this paper a reformulation of the usual set-theoretical semantics of the description logic $\mathcal{ALC}$ with general TBoxes by using categorical language. In this setting, $\mathcal{ALC}$ concepts are represented as objects, concept subsumptions as arrows, and memberships as logical quantifiers over objects and arrows of categories. Such a category-based semantics provides a more modular representation of the semantics of $\mathcal{ALC}$. This feature allows us to define a sublogic of $\mathcal{ALC}$ by dropping the interaction between existential and universal restrictions, which would be responsible for an exponential complexity in space. Such a sublogic is undefinable in the usual set-theoretical semantics, We show that this sublogic is {\sc{PSPACE}} by proposing a deterministic algorithm for checking concept satisfiability which runs in polynomial space. 我们在本文中提出了使用分类语言用通用 TBox 重新表述描述逻辑 $\mathcal{ALC}$ 的常用集合论语义。在此设置中，$\mathcal{ALC}$ 概念表示为对象，概念包含表示为箭头，而成员资格表示为对象和类别箭头上的逻辑量词。这种基于类别的语义提供了 $\mathcal{ALC}$ 语义的更加模块化的表示。此功能允许我们通过删除存在限制和普遍限制之间的相互作用来定义 $\mathcal{ALC}$ 的子逻辑，这将导致空间的指数复杂性。这样的子逻辑在通常的集合论语义中是无法定义的。我们通过提出一种用于检查在多项式空间中运行的概念可满足性的确定性算法来证明该子逻辑是 {\sc{PSPACE}}。 本文将描述逻辑ALC的集合论语义转换为范畴语义，并基于此提出一种新的子逻辑以降低推理复杂度。 Ludovic Brieulle,Chan Le Duc,Pascal Vaillant cs.LO,cs.AI

authors_text: Wenlong Zhang,Guangyuan Shi,Yihao Liu,Chao Dong,Xiao-Ming Wu
title: A Closer Look at Blind Super-Resolution: Degradation Models, Baselines, and Performance Upper Bounds
title_zh: 仔细研究盲超分辨率：退化模型、基线和性能上限
comment: Accepted by CVPR Workshop, NTIRE 2022
comments: Accepted by CVPR Workshop, NTIRE 2022
abstract: Degradation models play an important role in Blind super-resolution (SR). The classical degradation model, which mainly involves blur degradation, is too simple to simulate real-world scenarios. The recently proposed practical degradation model includes a full spectrum of degradation types, but only considers complex cases that use all degradation types in the degradation process, while ignoring many important corner cases that are common in the real world. To address this problem, we propose a unified gated degradation model to generate a broad set of degradation cases using a random gate controller. Based on the gated degradation model, we propose simple baseline networks that can effectively handle non-blind, classical, practical degradation cases as well as many other corner cases. To fairly evaluate the performance of our baseline networks against state-of-the-art methods and understand their limits, we introduce the performance upper bound of an SR network for every degradation type. Our empirical analysis shows that with the unified gated degradation model, the proposed baselines can achieve much better performance than existing methods in quantitative and qualitative results, which are close to the performance upper bounds.
summary: Degradation models play an important role in Blind super-resolution (SR). The classical degradation model, which mainly involves blur degradation, is too simple to simulate real-world scenarios. The recently proposed practical degradation model includes a full spectrum of degradation types, but only considers complex cases that use all degradation types in the degradation process, while ignoring many important corner cases that are common in the real world. To address this problem, we propose a unified gated degradation model to generate a broad set of degradation cases using a random gate controller. Based on the gated degradation model, we propose simple baseline networks that can effectively handle non-blind, classical, practical degradation cases as well as many other corner cases. To fairly evaluate the performance of our baseline networks against state-of-the-art methods and understand their limits, we introduce the performance upper bound of an SR network for every degradation type. Our empirical analysis shows that with the unified gated degradation model, the proposed baselines can achieve much better performance than existing methods in quantitative and qualitative results, which are close to the performance upper bounds.
abstract_zh: 退化模型在盲超分辨率（SR）中发挥着重要作用。经典的退化模型主要涉及模糊退化，过于简单，无法模拟真实场景。最近提出的实用退化模型包括全谱的退化类型，但仅考虑退化过程中使用所有退化类型的复杂情况，而忽略了现实世界中常见的许多重要的极端情况。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一个统一的门控退化模型，使用随机门控制器生成广泛的退化案例。基于门控退化模型，我们提出了简单的基线网络，可以有效处理非盲、经典、实际退化案例以及许多其他极端情况。为了根据最先进的方法公平地评估我们的基线网络的性能并了解其局限性，我们引入了每种退化类型的 SR 网络的性能上限。我们的实证分析表明，通过统一的门控退化模型，所提出的基线在定量和定性结果上比现有方法可以获得更好的性能，接近性能上限。

AI.tldr: 本文提出统一的门控退化模型和简单基线网络，以解决盲超分辨率中退化模型不全面的问题，并引入性能上限评估方法。
AI.motivation: 现有退化模型无法全面模拟真实场景，经典模型过于简单，实用模型忽略常见边缘情况，导致现有方法在复杂退化场景下表现不足。
AI.method: 设计随机门控控制器生成多样化退化案例，提出可处理多种退化类型的简单基线网络，并为每种退化类型定义性能上限作为评估基准。
AI.result: 新基线在定量和定性指标上均优于现有方法，性能接近理论上限，验证了模型的有效性和泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 门控退化模型与基线方法显著提升了盲超分辨率的鲁棒性，为后续研究提供了更全面的评估框架和改进方向。

authors_text: Yimin Xu,Mingbao Lin,Hong Yang,Fei Chao,Rongrong Ji
title: Shadow-Aware Dynamic Convolution for Shadow Removal
title_zh: 用于阴影去除的阴影感知动态卷积
abstract: With a wide range of shadows in many collected images, shadow removal has aroused increasing attention since uncontaminated images are of vital importance for many downstream multimedia tasks. Current methods consider the same convolution operations for both shadow and non-shadow regions while ignoring the large gap between the color mappings for the shadow region and the non-shadow region, leading to poor quality of reconstructed images and a heavy computation burden. To solve this problem, this paper introduces a novel plug-and-play Shadow-Aware Dynamic Convolution (SADC) module to decouple the interdependence between the shadow region and the non-shadow region. Inspired by the fact that the color mapping of the non-shadow region is easier to learn, our SADC processes the non-shadow region with a lightweight convolution module in a computationally cheap manner and recovers the shadow region with a more complicated convolution module to ensure the quality of image reconstruction. Given that the non-shadow region often contains more background color information, we further develop a novel intra-convolution distillation loss to strengthen the information flow from the non-shadow region to the shadow region. Extensive experiments on the ISTD and SRD datasets show our method achieves better performance in shadow removal over many state-of-the-arts. Our code is available at https://github.com/xuyimin0926/SADC.
summary: With a wide range of shadows in many collected images, shadow removal has aroused increasing attention since uncontaminated images are of vital importance for many downstream multimedia tasks. Current methods consider the same convolution operations for both shadow and non-shadow regions while ignoring the large gap between the color mappings for the shadow region and the non-shadow region, leading to poor quality of reconstructed images and a heavy computation burden. To solve this problem, this paper introduces a novel plug-and-play Shadow-Aware Dynamic Convolution (SADC) module to decouple the interdependence between the shadow region and the non-shadow region. Inspired by the fact that the color mapping of the non-shadow region is easier to learn, our SADC processes the non-shadow region with a lightweight convolution module in a computationally cheap manner and recovers the shadow region with a more complicated convolution module to ensure the quality of image reconstruction. Given that the non-shadow region often contains more background color information, we further develop a novel intra-convolution distillation loss to strengthen the information flow from the non-shadow region to the shadow region. Extensive experiments on the ISTD and SRD datasets show our method achieves better performance in shadow removal over many state-of-the-arts. Our code is available at https://github.com/xuyimin0926/SADC.
abstract_zh: 由于许多收集的图像中存在广泛的阴影，阴影去除引起了越来越多的关注，因为未受污染的图像对于许多下游多媒体任务至关重要。当前的方法考虑对阴影和非阴影区域进行相同的卷积操作，而忽略了阴影区域和非阴影区域的颜色映射之间的大差距，导致重建图像的质量差和计算负担重。为了解决这个问题，本文引入了一种新颖的即插即用阴影感知动态卷积（SADC）模块来解耦阴影区域和非阴影区域之间的相互依赖性。受非阴影区域的颜色映射更容易学习这一事实的启发，我们的SADC以计算成本低的方式使用轻量级卷积模块处理非阴影区域，并使用更复杂的卷积模块恢复阴影区域，以确保图像重建的质量。鉴于非阴影区域通常包含更多的背景颜色信息，我们进一步开发了一种新颖的卷积内蒸馏损失，以加强从非阴影区域到阴影区域的信息流。对 ISTD 和 SRD 数据集的大量实验表明，我们的方法在阴影去除方面比许多最先进的方法取得了更好的性能。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/xuyimin0926/SADC 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Shadow-Aware Dynamic Convolution (SADC)的模块，用于改进阴影去除任务中的图像重建质量并减少计算负担。
AI.motivation: 现有阴影去除方法对阴影和非阴影区域使用相同的卷积操作，忽略了两者在颜色映射上的显著差异，导致重建图像质量差且计算量大。
AI.method: 设计了一种可插拔的SADC模块，通过轻量卷积处理非阴影区域并用复杂卷积恢复阴影区域，同时引入了基于非阴影区域信息流的卷积蒸馏损失。
AI.result: 在ISTD和SRD数据集上的实验表明，该方法在阴影去除任务中优于许多现有先进方法，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: SADC模块通过解耦阴影与非阴影区域的依赖关系，并利用信息流增强机制，有效提升了阴影去除的性能。
